headline: Kirkwood Pulse • April 27, 2026
overview: Shrewsbury has joined a feasibility study to explore formal collaboration with nearby municipalities for fire and emergency services to improve sustainability and reduce costs amid fragmented county services and financial challenges faced by smaller departments. The Missouri History Museum in Forest Park is hosting a free four-day Route 66 centennial festival from April 30 to May 3, featuring live music, classic car displays, family activities, and historical talks that highlight the region's significant role in the highway's history, offering educational and recreational opportunities for local residents. The National Shrine of Our Lady of the Miraculous Medal in Perryville, Missouri, founded in 1818 as the first higher education institution west of the Mississippi, offers free guided tours and a serene environment for reflection, highlighting its historical and cultural significance to the local community.

items.title: Strength In Numbers
items.excerpt: Every year, the lone fire station in Shrewsbury responds to more than 2,400 emergency calls, often in support of neighboring communities in St. Louis County. Officials in the city of 6,400 residents now are exploring a more formal administrative and financial collaboration with peer municipalities as part of a feasibility study initiated by nearby Clayton. The study addresses the fragmented
items.ai_summary: Shrewsbury has joined a feasibility study to explore formal collaboration with nearby municipalities for fire and emergency services to improve sustainability and reduce costs amid fragmented county services and financial challenges faced by smaller departments.

items.title: Route 66 Festival
items.excerpt: The Missouri History Museum in Forest Park will host a celebration to mark the 100th anniversary of Route 66 with a four-day festival, April 30 through May 3. items.ai_summary: The Missouri History Museum in Forest Park is hosting a free four-day Route 66 centennial festival from April 30 to May 3, featuring live music, classic car displays, family activities, and historical talks that highlight the region's significant role in the highway's history, offering educational and recreational opportunities for local residents.

items.title: A Place For Peace & Reflection
items.excerpt: Visit Perryville, Missouri, for the National Shrine of Our Lady of the Miraculous Medal, 1805 W. Saint Joseph St. This peaceful place was founded in 1818 with the construction of a small log church and seminary, which served as the first institution for higher education west of the Mississippi River. items.ai_summary: The National Shrine of Our Lady of the Miraculous Medal in Perryville, Missouri, founded in 1818 as the first higher education institution west of the Mississippi, offers free guided tours and a serene environment for reflection, highlighting its historical and cultural significance to the local community.

items.title: Stunning Sunset Ridge Cabin
items.excerpt: Just a two-hour drive away from St. Louis is the perfect location for a great summer getaway or a girls’ weekend. Sunset Ridge Cabin is a three-level cabin that sleeps eight comfortably. Located on the Black River south of Farmington near Lesterville, Sunset Ridge is near many outdoor destinations including Johnson Shut-Ins, Elephant Rocks, Rocky Falls, Millstream Gardens, Pickle Springs,
items.ai_summary: Sunset Ridge Cabin near Farmington offers a spacious, fully equipped retreat for up to eight guests, providing easy access to popular outdoor sites in the Ozark Mountains, making it an ideal local getaway for St. Louis residents seeking nature and relaxation within a two-hour drive.

items.title: Spring Into Naperville, Illinois
items.excerpt: There’s plenty to do this spring in Naperville, Illinois. This historic town offers stunning natural scenery, fun activities, and plenty of shopping and food for the whole family. Naper Settlement is a family-friendly way to experience American history with special exhibits celebrating the 250th anniversary of America. Set across 13 acres, this interactive destination gives kids and adults room to
items.ai_summary: Naperville offers diverse spring activities including historical exhibits at Naper Settlement, unique recreation at Centennial Beach and Paddleboat Quarry, and vibrant dining at Block 59, enhancing local tourism and community engagement. Seasonal events like live music, trolley tours, and outdoor fitness classes provide residents and visitors with enriching…

items.title: Art On The Square Steps Beyond The Frame In 2026
items.excerpt: For one weekend each spring, something remarkable happens in Downtown Belleville. The streets fill with color. Conversations spark between strangers. Music drifts through the air. And art — once confined to studios and galleries — spills into the open, inviting everyone to be part of the experience. That transformation returns May 15-17, as Art on the Square celebrates its 24th
items.ai_summary: Art on the Square returns to Downtown Belleville May 15-17, 2026, featuring a new Local Artist Competition spotlighting regional talent alongside national artists, enhancing community connection and pride through diverse art forms, live music, and interactive experiences, all free and open to the public.
items.community_impact: Impact unclear based on automatically extracted text.

items.title: See The American Gothic House
items.excerpt: Less than four hours northwest of St. Louis is Eldon, Iowa, home of the famous American Gothic House. This Carpenter Gothic-style home is best known for its appearance in Grant Wood’s iconic painting, “American Gothic.” While the painting resides in Chicago, the house can be viewed all year long. The adjacent visitor’s center includes an exhibit gallery, gift shop, film
items.ai_summary: The American Gothic House in Eldon, Iowa, located less than four hours from St. Louis, is open year-round with a visitor center offering exhibits, events, and tours, providing a unique cultural experience tied to the iconic painting and enriching local tourism opportunities.

items.title: Lots To Do In Lexington, MO
items.excerpt: With a mix of old and new, there’s plenty to see and do in Lexington, Missouri. Catch the summer living history trolley tours every second Sunday, June through September. Each month’s theme will cover a different part of Lexington’s history. While there, follow the original Santa Fe Trail through town or download an audio driving tour, which takes drivers through
items.ai_summary: Lexington, MO offers summer living history trolley tours from June to September highlighting local history, along with scenic drives on the Old Trails Road and opportunities to explore historic neighborhoods, orchards, and local businesses, enhancing community engagement and tourism.
